binaries.title: mrxsmb10.sys (SMB 1.0 Redirector) - WIL feature-staging rollout of server-transport reference validation

changed_functions.name: MRxSmbHandOverSrvCall (unpatched 0x1C000FFD0, patched 0x1C000EFE0)
key_changes: Removed the WIL feature gate Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (checked at 0x1C001002F and 0x1C00102EC in the unpatched build)
SmbCepReferenceServerTransport (unpatched 0x1C0013598, patched 0x1C0012558) is now called unconditionally instead of only when the feature flag was enabled
SmbCepDereferenceServerTransport (unpatched 0x1C0013454, patched 0x1C0012414) is now called unconditionally on cleanup instead of only when the feature flag was enabled and the transport was non-NULL

In both the feature-enabled and feature-disabled unpatched paths, and in the patched build, the SAME server-transport pointer P is stored at SrvCall+0x140 and passed to SmbCeUpdateTransportDispatchVectors; there is no pointer-indirection difference
changed_functions.security_notes: No security impact. The unpatched code branches on the WIL feature flag Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (this is a feature-staging gate, NOT an SMB2 availability check). On the feature-enabled path SmbCepReferenceServerTransport reads the transport pointer P from the argument slot, validates it (P != NULL and state field [P+0x0C] == 0, else returns 0xC000020C), takes a transient reference (lock xadd [P+8],1), and returns P. On the feature-disabled path the same pointer value P is loaded directly from the same argument slot (mov r15,[rbp+0x1E0]) without validation and without a transient reference. In both cases the identical pointer P is stored into SrvCall+0x140 and passed to SmbCeUpdateTransportDispatchVectors, so there is no type confusion and no one-level indirection error. The transient reference is released by SmbCepDereferenceServerTransport within the same function, so it is net-zero and introduces no persistent reference-count imbalance. The patch removes the feature gate so validation and the transient reference are always performed. This is defense-in-depth hardening (rejecting a torn-down transport and guarding the handover window against a concurrent free), but no reachable trigger for such a condition is demonstrated and the stored/used pointer is unchanged.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED MRxSmbHandOverSrvCall @ 0x1C000FFD0:
0x1C001002F: call    Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage ; WIL feature gate
0x1C0010034: test    eax, eax
0x1C0010036: jz      0x1C0010067            ; feature disabled -> direct-load path
0x1C0010044: call    SmbCepReferenceServerTransport ; enabled: validate + transient ref, out = P
0x1C0010049: mov     rsi, [rsp+2B0h+var_260]; rsi = P
0x1C0010067: mov     r15, [rbp+1B0h+arg_20] ; disabled: r15 = P (same slot value)
0x1C00102EC: call    Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1C00102F5: mov     [rbx+140h], rsi        ; enabled path stores P
0x1C00102FE: mov     [rbx+140h], r15        ; disabled path stores P (identical value)
0x1C0010305: mov     rcx, [rbx+140h]        ; rcx = P
0x1C0010311: lea     rdx, MRxSmbVctConnectionEventHandler
0x1C0010318: call    cs:__imp_SmbCeUpdateTransportDispatchVectors ; called with P
0x1C0010184: call    SmbCepDereferenceServerTransport ; only on feature-enabled cleanup

PATCHED MRxSmbHandOverSrvCall @ 0x1C000EFE0 (feature gate removed):
0x1C000F04C: call    SmbCepReferenceServerTransport ; unconditional, out = P
0x1C000F051: mov     r12, [rsp+2B0h+var_260]; r12 = P
0x1C000F2DC: mov     [rbx+140h], r12        ; store P (unconditional)
0x1C000F2E3: call    cs:__imp_SmbCeUpdateTransportDispatchVectors ; called with P
0x1C000F16B: call    SmbCepDereferenceServerTransport ; always

changed_functions.name: SmbExtSecuritySessionSetupExchangeFinalize (unpatched 0x1C0002F00, patched 0x1C0002780)
key_changes: Removed the Feature_Servicing_EnableSMBHardeningTelemetry__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage-gated ETW telemetry block (_tlgWriteTransfer_EtwWriteTransfer), reducing the function from 199 to 127 instructions
WPP trace GUID relocation and register allocation changes

changed_functions.name: SmbCeCompleteSessionEntryInitialization (unpatched 0x1C0041098, patched 0x1C003FEF8)
key_changes: Removed the Feature_Servicing_EnableSMBHardeningTelemetry telemetry gate before the ETW logging block
Register reallocation and WPP trace GUID / address relocations

changed_functions.name: DriverEntry (unpatched 0x1C00247D0, patched 0x1C0023700)
key_changes: Removed the Feature_Servicing_EnableSMBHardeningTelemetry telemetry gate before ETW logging
WPP trace GUID and address relocations; wil_InitializeFeatureStaging / wil_UninitializeFeatureStaging remain in both builds

security_findings.entry_point: SMB server-call handover dispatch, invoked by the RDBSS framework during SMB server connection establishment (user-mode SMB tree connect via NtCreateFile / WNetAddConnection2 to \\server\share)
call_chain: RDBSS SrvCall dispatch (user-mode SMB tree connect)
Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage selects code path (runtime WIL feature flag, not attacker input)
SmbCeUpdateTransportDispatchVectors called with the server-transport pointer
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change (WIL feature-staging rollout; defense-in-depth hardening of server-transport reference validation)
security_findings.description: The unpatched MRxSmbHandOverSrvCall branches on the WIL feature flag Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage. When enabled, SmbCepReferenceServerTransport reads the server-transport pointer P from the argument slot, validates it (P != NULL and state field [P+0x0C] == 0, else 0xC000020C), takes a transient reference (lock xadd [P+8],1), and returns P. When disabled, the same pointer P is loaded directly from the same slot (mov r15,[rbp+0x1E0]) without validation and without a transient reference. In both paths, and in the patched build, the identical pointer P is stored at SrvCall+0x140 and passed to SmbCeUpdateTransportDispatchVectors. There is no type confusion and no arg5-vs-*arg5 indirection difference: SmbCepReferenceServerTransport returns the value of the argument slot, which is exactly what the direct-load path uses. The transient reference is released by SmbCepDereferenceServerTransport within the same function (net-zero), so no persistent reference-count imbalance exists in either build. The patch removes the feature gate so the validation and transient reference are always performed. This is a hardening/servicing change with no demonstrable reachable primitive.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: MRxSmbHandOverSrvCall runs during SMB server-call handover when a client establishes a connection to \\server\share. Which unpatched path executes is determined solely by the runtime state of the WIL feature flag Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage, not by attacker-controlled input. Because both paths store and use the same transport pointer P, reaching either path does not produce a distinct corruptible memory operation.
security_findings.primitive: None. The pointer stored at SrvCall+0x140 and passed to SmbCeUpdateTransportDispatchVectors is the same valid server-transport object pointer in both builds and both feature paths. No memory corruption, no type confusion, and no reference-count imbalance / use-after-free is introduced or removed by this diff.
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED MRxSmbHandOverSrvCall @ 0x1C000FFD0:

0x1C001002F: call    Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage ; WIL feature gate (NOT an SMB2 check)
0x1C0010034: test    eax, eax
0x1C0010036: jz      0x1C0010067            ; feature disabled -> direct-load path
0x1C0010038: lea     rdx, [rsp+2B0h+var_260]
0x1C001003D: lea     rcx, [rbp+1B0h+arg_20] ; &arg5_slot
0x1C0010044: call    SmbCepReferenceServerTransport ; validate + transient ref; out = P
0x1C0010049: mov     rsi, [rsp+2B0h+var_260]; rsi = P
0x1C001004E: mov     r15d, edi              ; r15 = 0
0x1C0010067: mov     r15, [rbp+1B0h+arg_20] ; disabled path: r15 = P (value of same slot)
0x1C00102EC: call    Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1C00102F1: test    eax, eax
0x1C00102F3: jz      0x1C00102FE
0x1C00102F5: mov     [rbx+140h], rsi        ; enabled path stores P
0x1C00102FE: mov     [rbx+140h], r15        ; disabled path stores P (identical value)
0x1C0010305: mov     rcx, [rbx+140h]        ; rcx = P
0x1C001030C: test    rcx, rcx
0x1C001030F: jz      0x1C0010324
0x1C0010311: lea     rdx, MRxSmbVctConnectionEventHandler
0x1C0010318: call    cs:__imp_SmbCeUpdateTransportDispatchVectors ; called with P

; cleanup (transient ref released only on feature-enabled path):
0x1C0010171: call    Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1C0010176: test    eax, eax
0x1C0010178: jz      0x1C0010189
0x1C001017A: test    rsi, rsi
0x1C001017D: jz      0x1C0010189
0x1C001017F: lea     rcx, [rsp+2B0h+var_260]
0x1C0010184: call    SmbCepDereferenceServerTransport

SmbCepReferenceServerTransport @ 0x1C0013598:
0x1C00135D0: mov     rax, [rsi]             ; rax = *(&arg5_slot) = P
0x1C00135D3: test    rax, rax
0x1C00135D6: jz      0x1C001363E            ; NULL -> 0xC000020C
0x1C00135D8: cmp     [rax+0Ch], ebx         ; reject if state field [P+0x0C] != 0
0x1C00135DB: jnz     0x1C001363E
0x1C00135E3: lock xadd [rax+8], r9d         ; transient reference: refcount++ at [P+8]
0x1C0013636: mov     rax, [rsi]             ; rax = P
0x1C0013639: mov     [rdi], rax             ; output = P (same pointer the direct-load path uses)

breakpoints.why: Entry point. rcx=arg1 (server-entry context), rdx=arg2, r8=arg3, r9=arg4 (SrvCall output), [rsp+0x28]=arg5 (server-transport pointer slot).

call Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage. eax==0 selects the direct-load path; eax!=0 selects the SmbCepReferenceServerTransport path. This is a WIL feature flag, not an SMB2 availability check.
mov r15,[rbp+0x1E0]: loads the server-transport pointer P from the arg5 slot on the feature-disabled path. r15 here equals rsi on the enabled path (0x1C0010049) - the same pointer P.
mov [rbx+0x140],r15: stores P into SrvCall+0x140 on the feature-disabled path. Compare with the enabled-path store of rsi at 0x1C00102F5 - both write the same value P.
call SmbCeUpdateTransportDispatchVectors. rcx = [rbx+0x140] = P, identical on both paths. No type-confused pointer is passed.

watch: eax after 0x1C0010034: 0 selects the direct-load path, non-zero selects the SmbCepReferenceServerTransport path
r15 at 0x1C0010067 vs rsi at 0x1C0010049: both hold the same server-transport pointer P
[rbx+0x140] after the store: equals P on both paths
rcx at 0x1C0010318: equals P on both paths
[P+8]: transient reference count, incremented on the feature-enabled path and decremented again by SmbCepDereferenceServerTransport within the same function
key_offsets: 0x1C001002F: call Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage - WIL feature gate selecting the two paths
0x1C0010067: mov r15,[rbp+0x1E0] - direct load of P on the feature-disabled path
0x1C0010044 / 0x1C00102EC: SmbCepReferenceServerTransport call and second feature check
0x1C00102F5 / 0x1C00102FE: store of P into SrvCall+0x140 (rsi vs r15, same value)
0x1C0010184: SmbCepDereferenceServerTransport (releases the transient reference on the enabled path)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Not a vulnerability trigger. MRxSmbHandOverSrvCall runs during SMB server-call handover on a client connecting to \\server\share. The chosen path in the unpatched build depends only on the runtime state of the WIL feature flag Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: On both the feature-enabled and feature-disabled paths, r15/rsi, [rbx+0x140], and rcx at the SmbCeUpdateTransportDispatchVectors call all hold the same valid server-transport pointer P. No corruption, page fault, or type-confused pointer occurs. The only behavioral difference is the presence (enabled) or absence (disabled) of the non-null / not-torn-down validation and the transient reference on [P+8].
debugging_aids.notes: SmbCepReferenceServerTransport (unpatched 0x1C0013598, patched 0x1C0012558) reads *arg1 (the value of the arg5 slot), validates it, increments the transient reference at offset +8, and returns that same value. It does not add a level of indirection. SmbCepDereferenceServerTransport (unpatched 0x1C0013454, patched 0x1C0012414) releases that transient reference. The patch removes the Feature_84197691 gate so both are always executed. Image base: use 'lm m mrxsmb10' and add the offsets (default base 0x1C0000000).

summary: No security-relevant change. In MRxSmbHandOverSrvCall the unpatched build branches on the WIL feature flag Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (a feature-staging gate, not an SMB2 availability check). The feature-enabled path validates the server-transport pointer and takes a transient reference via SmbCepReferenceServerTransport; the feature-disabled path loads the same pointer directly. Both paths store the identical transport pointer at SrvCall+0x140 and pass it to SmbCeUpdateTransportDispatchVectors, so there is no type confusion and no pointer-indirection difference. The transient reference is released within the same function, so no persistent reference-count imbalance exists. The patch removes the feature gate so validation and the transient reference are always performed, and drops 21 WIL feature-staging / usage-reporting helper functions; three other functions lose only a Feature_Servicing_EnableSMBHardeningTelemetry-gated ETW telemetry block. This is a feature-staging rollout and defense-in-depth hardening with no demonstrable reachable primitive.
